name: Laura Fine
bio: Laura Fine is an Illinois state senator representing the 9th district — the same seat once held by Daniel Biss. A former journalist, she is running for the open IL-09 congressional seat. She has raised approximately $2M. (Source: FEC H6IL09194; Wikipedia)

jacket.note: ~$2M raised through Dec 31, 2025 per FEC filings.

donors.name: Gessner family (documented AIPAC-network donors)
donors.amount_note: Confirmed AIPAC-network donation per Evanston Roundtable investigative reporting

donors.name: Michael Tuchin (AIPAC board president — hosted fundraiser)
donors.amount_note: AIPAC board president hosted fundraiser for Fine; personal contribution amount requires FEC lookup

donors.name: Elect Chicago Women Super PAC (AIPAC-affiliated outside spending)
donors.amount_note: AIPAC-affiliated Super PAC spent $1M+ on ads supporting Fine. This is OUTSIDE spending — NOT in campaign committee total of $2,556,808.

donors.name: Out-of-state donors — Florida
donors.amount_note: Largest out-of-state contribution bloc per Daily Northwestern FEC analysis

donors.name: Out-of-state donors — New York
donors.amount_note: Second largest out-of-state bloc per Daily Northwestern FEC analysis

jacket.donors_note: Donors list is PARTIAL — only notable/flagged donors are itemized. total_raised ($2,000,000) reflects the full FEC aggregate total (H6IL09194). Full donor breakdown pending ETL import. The Gessner family entry represents $14,000 of documented AIPAC-network contributions; remaining $1,986,000 is unitemized in this record.

red_flags.label: AIPAC-Connected Donors Flooding Campaign
red_flags.detail: Evanston Now reported in October 2025 that AIPAC-linked donors are actively funding Fine's IL-09 campaign, including $14,000 from the Gessner family, documented AIPAC donors. WBEZ reported in February 2026 that pro-Israel groups and affiliates have poured $13.7M into Chicago-area primaries. Additionally, the AIPAC-affiliated "Elect Chicago Women" Super PAC spent $1M+ on ads supporting Fine, and AIPAC board president Michael Tuchin hosted a fundraiser for her campaign.

key_votes.bill: Illinois Mental Health Early Action on Campus Act (SB 2792), 2020
key_votes.summary: Fine sponsored law requiring Illinois public colleges to adopt mental health early action policies. Signed 2020.
Fine passed law extending prior authorization bans to cover medically necessary outpatient mental health services — major insurance reform expanding mental health access in Illinois.

key_votes.bill: Illinois PFAS (forever chemicals) firefighting foam phase-out, 2023
key_votes.summary: Fine sponsored legislation to phase out PFAS chemicals from firefighting foam used in Illinois.
Fine introduced bill to ban styrofoam food containers in Illinois starting 2030. Exempts egg cartons. As of March 2026, not yet enacted.

career_history.highlight: Senate Mental Health Committee; behavioral health policy leader

policy_platform.position: Lead advocate for expanded mental health funding in Illinois; authored mental health parity legislation.
Supports increased K-12 and higher education funding; backed Evidence-Based Funding formula.
Consistent votes for environmental protection legislation; supports clean energy transition.

social_pulse.summary: Low public profile outside her district. Positive among mental health advocacy community. No major controversies.

trust_indicators.label: Mental health policy champion in Illinois Senate
